title: Daily Social Rhythm Checklist
description: Track your daily routine anchors to build stability. Research shows that consistent social rhythms — regular sleep, meals, activity, and social contact — reduce the risk of mood episodes in bipolar disorder. This checklist helps you notice which anchors you're maintaining and which ones need attention.
instructions: Review this checklist daily (ideally in the evening). Check off the anchors you maintained today. The goal is not perfection — it's consistency over time. If you notice several anchors slipping for multiple days, consider what's disrupting your routine and whether you need to adjust.

items.label: I woke up within 30 minutes of my target wake time
I got into bed within 30 minutes of my target bedtime
I avoided screens and stimulating activities in the hour before bed
I ate breakfast or a morning meal at a regular time
I ate lunch at a regular time
I ate dinner at a regular time
I did some physical activity today (walk, exercise, stretching — any amount counts)
I spent time in natural daylight (especially morning light)
I avoided caffeine after my cutoff time (typically early afternoon)
I had at least one meaningful social interaction today (in person, phone, or video)
I had some quiet time for myself today
I took all prescribed medications at the right times
I checked in with myself about my mood, energy, and sleep today
I avoided alcohol, recreational drugs, or other destabilizing substances

disclaimer: This checklist supports daily routine stability as part of bipolar disorder self-management. It is not a substitute for professional treatment. If you notice your routines breaking down significantly or your mood becoming unstable, contact your treatment team. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
